# Principle 1: Progressive Disclosure


> **Split Skill information into three layers. Do not cram everything into a single file.**

When Claude Code starts a session, metadata from every installed Skill is loaded into context. If a single SKILL.md holds 3,000 lines, all of it immediately consumes context — including everything Claude won't need for this task.

Thariq explains this directly in "Seeing like an Agent" using the Claude Code Guide Subagent example: he wanted to teach Claude how to use Claude Code itself. He could have stuffed it all into the system prompt, but that causes "context rot" and interferes with the primary job of writing code. Instead, he used Progressive Disclosure — a subagent that Claude calls only when needed.

Applied to Skills:

| Layer | Location | Contents | When Loaded |
|-------|----------|----------|-------------|
| Metadata | `skill.yaml` description | One-sentence trigger condition | Always |
| Core | `SKILL.md` (target 300 lines, hard limit 500) | Key directives + Gotchas + links to other files | When Skill is invoked |
| Supplementary | `references/api.md`, `assets/` | Detailed API signatures, templates, example code | When Claude needs it |

**Bad**: A single `SKILL.md` that crams API reference, example code, setup guide, and troubleshooting into 3,000 lines.

**Good**: `SKILL.md` targets 300 lines with only core directives and Gotchas. Detailed API reference lives in `references/api.md`, linked from SKILL.md. Never exceed 500 lines. Claude reads the linked files only when it needs them.

---
